description: Kredit Lab Bank Statement Classification Rulebook v3.5.2. Machine-readable rules for 27 transaction categories (C01-C27). Serves three execution modes from ONE document: Full AI, Optimised AI, Full Code. Company-agnostic AND bank-agnostic — uses variables (company_name, related_parties[], known_factoring_entities[]) and pattern rules that work across CIMB, Maybank/Maybank Islamic, Alliance, RHB, Public Bank, Bank Islam, Bank Rakyat, Hong Leong, Ambank, Affin, Agro, Muamalat, OCBC, UOB. v3.5.2 CHANGES (2026-05-01): C07 SOCSO regex/keywords aligned with parser core_utils._STATUTORY_PATTERNS (commit 6afb133). Added KESELAMA-truncation variant, FPX-B2B-anchored PERTUBUH alternate, and bare 'PERTUBUH CP_xxx' alternate (RHB-style 14-char truncation). statutory_truncated_forms.SOCSO list extended. Bucket-trust shortcut (statutory_bucket_trust_v3_4) remains primary; this regex widening only fires under paranoid-AI fallback re-regex. Cross-bank exclusions intact: PERTUBUHAN PELADANG / PELANCONGAN / NELAYAN / BUKAN KERAJAAN do not match. v3.5.1 CHANGES (2026-04-26, Sprint 6 #15): C24 Bank Fees & Charges — added 7 RHB-specific fee patterns to keywords[] and regex (RFLX INSTANT TRF SC, RFLX / CMxxx, REFLEX- / CMxxx, CHQ SVC, SERVICE CASH CHQ, SERVICE CHARGES-OTHERS, BANKERS REFER CHARGES) per project principle that any bank-charged service fee routes to C24. Parser layer (app.py) routes these to BANK FEES bucket with amount-safety check (auto-route only when amount ≤ RM 100, mirroring existing OTHER TRANSFER FEE rule); rules layer kept in sync. v3.5 CHANGES (2026-04-23, Sprint 4.5 Option A): (1) account_type enum collapsed to Current / Savings / OD — Cash Line and Cash Line-i display variants removed. Islamic Cashline-i / CAP-i / SAP-i is the same revolving-credit facility as conventional Overdraft and displays as OD. (2) schema_version bumped 6.3.4 → 6.3.5. v3.4 CHANGES (2026-04-23): (1) NEW C26 Trade Income / C27 Trade Expense — third-party company CR/DR previously falling to Unclassified now bucketed as trade activity (fixes Muhafiz RM 11.8M blind spot); (2) statutory_bucket trust — when parser emits transaction-level statutory_bucket field, classifier routes C06-C09 off that directly (no re-regex); (3) truncated_forms[] added to statutory keyword lists to handle FPX 20-char truncation (KUMPULAN WANG SIMPAN, PERTUBUHAN KESELAMAT, LEMBAGA HASIL DAL, PEMBANGUNAN SUMBER MANU); (4) C10 disambiguation — forbidden_preceded_by list blocks BA/TL/TF/FD/CT short forms when the token follows BIN/BINTI/A/L/A/P/BT/BTE (patronymic guard); (5) stop_words_drop_as_counterparty expanded; (6) EPF dual-band 11-15% OR 20-26% (Malaysia combined-remittance is standard); (7) STRUCTURAL ratio status added (≥4 consecutive months outside band). v3.3.1 CHANGES (2026-04-21): C05 commission default regular expense, C06-C09 statutory side-gate, Top 10 parties, CN6 ghost-verb suppression, Coverage % intersection + cap [0,100], effective_match_rate. v3.3 CHANGE: C04 removed from net debit exclusion.

global_rules.classification_order_note: C25 filters balance rows FIRST. Then own-party (C01/C02), salary (C05), related party (C03/C04), statutory (C06-C09), loan (C10-C11), FD/reversal (C12-C13), returned cheques (C14-C16), cash/cheque (C17-C20), bank fees (C24). C21-C23 are monitoring flags applied after classification. Remainder = income (CR) or expense (DR).
global_rules.no_unknown_bucket: If no exclusion matches, transaction is income (credit side) or expense (debit side). It stays in net credits/debits. No 'unknown' or 'unclassified' exclusion category.

dual_tagging.description: A single transaction can carry two category tags (e.g. C02 + C11). Excluded from net debits only ONCE via C02. C11 tracked separately for reporting.
dual_tagging.example: TR IBG MUHAFIZ SECURITY SDN Term loan = C02 (own party debit) + C11 (loan repayment)

jompay_rule.description: JomPAY is a payment channel, NOT a payee. Never classify based on JomPAY biller code alone. Classify ONLY when entity name is visible in description. If description is just 'JOMPAY xxxx:xxxxx' with no entity name → leave as regular expense.

fx_default.description: Default to NOT FX unless clear conversion evidence exists. TT CREDIT = transfer method, NOT currency indicator. RENTAS/JANM = domestic MYR. Alphabet codes (GBPV, USDP) in reference fields = internal voucher codes, NOT currencies.
true_fx_indicators: TT or TELEGRAPHIC TRANSFER with foreign source/destination AND conversion evidence
Explicit FOREX, FX CONV, or FOREIGN EXCHANGE in description
SWIFT/international wire with foreign currency amounts
Foreign beneficiary with international address and non-MYR amounts
not_fx: IBG with voucher codes (GBPV, USDP = voucher codes, not currencies)
Any MYR-to-MYR transfer between Malaysian entities

execution_modes.FULL_CODE: Works in all three modes. Deterministic keyword/regex/math rules.
execution_modes.AI_ASSIST: Needs AI in Full AI and Optimised AI modes. Exhaustive rules needed for Full Code mode.
execution_modes.HUMAN_REVIEW: Requires human analyst review. Cannot be fully automated.

unclassified_handling.description: Transactions with descriptions too vague or missing to determine type. Stays in net credits/debits — NOT excluded.
unclassified_handling.individual_listing_note: List individual unclassified transactions in separate array when single transaction exceeds threshold.

deterministic_eod.description: v3.2: EOD balance computed deterministically. Walk transactions in statement order, group by date, EOD = last transaction's running balance per date. Only dates with actual transactions count — no carry-forward for weekends/holidays.

metrics.eod_lowest: MIN(all daily EOD balances in the month)
metrics.eod_highest: MAX(all daily EOD balances in the month)
metrics.eod_average: SUM(all daily EOD balances) / COUNT(distinct dates with transactions)

rules: Only dates with transactions count — weekends/holidays with no activity are NOT included
Opening balance date: if belongs to previous month, do NOT include in current month's EOD
Closing balance is implicit — equals EOD of last transaction date. Do not double-count.
Opening balance on 1st with no other transactions that day = EOD for that date

statutory_bucket_trust_v3_4.description: v3.4 NEW. Parser may emit transaction-level `statutory_bucket` field (KWSP/SOCSO/LHDN/HRDF/null). When present, classifier MUST trust it and route C06-C09 directly — do NOT re-regex the raw description. Eliminates FPX 20-char truncation bugs.
statutory_bucket_trust_v3_4.routing: if tx.statutory_bucket == 'KWSP' → C06; == 'SOCSO' → C07; == 'LHDN' → C08; == 'HRDF' → C09 (all subject to side==DR AND NOT match_own_party gate).
statutory_bucket_trust_v3_4.fallback: If statutory_bucket absent, fall back to the keyword + truncated_forms regex list under each C06-C09 category.

statutory_truncated_forms.description: v3.4 NEW. FPX rails truncate descriptions at ~20 chars; these prefix matches are sufficient signals when the full form is cut off. Use in addition to the full phrase — match either form.

patronymic_guard_v3_4.description: v3.4 NEW. Parser emits `_patronymic_ambiguous_tokens: ['BA']` (or TL/TF/FD/CT) when the 2-letter token follows BIN/BINTI/A/L/A/P/BT/BTE/S/O/D/O — it is a name fragment, not a banking acronym. SKIP short-form rules on that row.
patronymic_guard_v3_4.note: Full-phrase forms (BANKERS ACCEPTANCE, TERM LOAN, FIXED DEPOSIT) still fire normally. Only the 2-letter short-form rules are gated by this guard.

stop_words_drop_as_counterparty.description: v3.4 NEW. Extended cross-bank list. If a cleaned counterparty_name after CN1-CN7 / TR1-TR4 / M1-M7 normalisation is equal to ANY of these tokens or all tokens are members of these lists, drop the counterparty (treat as parser dropout; not emitted in top_parties).

net_credits.formula: gross_credits - own_party_cr(C01) - related_party_cr(C03) - reversal_cr(C13) - loan_disbursement_cr(C10) - fd_interest_cr(C12) - inward_return_cr(C16)

net_debits.v3_3_change: C04 (related_party_dr) removed from exclusion. Related party debits stay in net debits for conservative credit assessment. C04 is reporting only — tagged and listed but not excluded.

validation_checks.check: Net Credits formula: gross - exclusions = net (exact)
Net Debits formula: gross_debits - own_party_dr(C02) = net_debits (exact). v3.3: C04 no longer in exclusion.
EPF / Salary should be 11-13% PER MONTH (where BOTH exist). WARNING if outside 8-16%. CATCH_UP if > 16%. NEVER use aggregate ratio.
SOCSO / Salary should be 2-3% PER MONTH (where BOTH exist). WARNING if outside 1-5%. CATCH_UP if > 5%. NEVER use aggregate ratio.
C02+C11 dual-tag excludes from net debits ONCE via C02
Sum of monthly net_credits = consolidated net_credits

EPF coverage (v3.3.1 UPDATED, cross-bank): every month with salary_paid > 0 should have statutory_epf > 0. Coverage formula MUST use set intersection, bounded [0,100]: covered = epf_months_list ∩ salary_months_list; epf_coverage_pct = min(len(covered)/len(salary_months_list)*100, 100) if salary_months_list else 0. Do NOT use raw epf_months_paid/salary_months_active — produces >100% when EPF pays in a non-payroll month (MYTUTOR LHDN 120% bug).

SOCSO coverage (v3.3.1 UPDATED, cross-bank): every month with salary_paid > 0 should have statutory_socso > 0. Same intersection formula as V7: covered = socso_months_list ∩ salary_months_list; socso_coverage_pct = min(len(covered)/len(salary_months_list)*100, 100).

LHDN presence (v3.3.1 REFRAMED, DECOUPLED FROM SALARY): LHDN bucket captures multiple unrelated payment types — PCB/MTD salary withholding, CP204 corporate income tax instalments, SST, stamp duty, RPGT. Only PCB is strictly payroll-driven. Do NOT compute a coverage ratio against salary_months_active — the comparison mixes unrelated payment types and produced the >100% anomaly (MYTUTOR LHDN 6/5 = 120%). Instead, track lhdn_months_paid + total amount as an INFORMATIONAL presence check. Surface in observations if the company has active payroll (salary_months_active > 0) AND zero LHDN payments across the period — that's a potential PCB non-remittance concern. Otherwise report count and amount without a ratio.

HRDF presence (v3.3.1 REFRAMED, DECOUPLED FROM SALARY): HRDF (PSMB) is exempt for employers under certain headcount/industry thresholds (typically <10 employees in many covered industries). A missing HRDF is NOT necessarily non-compliance. Do NOT compute a coverage ratio against salary_months_active. Track hrdf_months_paid + total amount as informational presence only. Large companies (salary_months_active > 0 AND estimated headcount high AND zero HRDF) may be flagged informationally but never as a hard failure.

Statutory payment gap summary (v3.3.1 UPDATED): aggregate V7 (EPF coverage), V8 (SOCSO coverage), V9 (LHDN presence), V9b (HRDF presence) into consolidated.statutory_compliance. Overall status: COMPLIANT / GAPS_DETECTED / CRITICAL. COMPLIANT = EPF and SOCSO coverage 100% (regardless of LHDN/HRDF). GAPS_DETECTED = EPF or SOCSO coverage < 100%. CRITICAL = EPF or SOCSO coverage = 0% despite active payroll. LHDN and HRDF absence alone never triggers a degradation from COMPLIANT (they are informational only in v3.3.1).

EOD computation: eod_lowest <= eod_average <= eod_highest for every month. Verify eod_average = SUM(daily EOD balances) / COUNT(distinct transaction dates). Deterministic method. OD-aware interpretation (account_type='OD'): eod_lowest numerically = LOWEST debt magnitude (BEST liquidity); eod_highest numerically = HIGHEST debt magnitude (WORST liquidity). Downstream credit-assessment flags should interpret OD EOD metrics accordingly — do not treat OD eod_lowest as a liquidity risk.

Related parties completeness: RP2 root-name scan must find ALL entities sharing company root. Report: company root word(s), number of entities found, their names. Auto-detected parties (RP7, RP8) must appear in report_info.related_parties[].

Loan repayment scope: C11 transactions must NOT include related party personal loans or bank transfer fees. Cross-check: every C11 transaction should have company_name in description OR be standalone facility repayment. Personal loan payments = C04 only. OTHER TRANSFER FEE = C24 only.

Statutory side-gate (C06-C09) — keyword match MUST be gated on side==DR AND NOT match_own_party(desc). Any CR-side statutory keyword (e.g. 'DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT EPF PAYMENT <company_name>') is an inter-account funding transfer (C01), NOT a statutory payment. Applies to all banks equally — own_party match uses FULL COMPANY NAME after normalisation, not bank-specific prefixes. Test: if side=='CR' or match_own_party(desc) returns true, statutory categories MUST NOT fire.

Ghost-verb suppression from top_parties (cross-bank): top_payers and top_payees MUST NOT contain counterparty names that are only a payment-rail prefix with no entity name attached. See counterparty_extraction_rules.normalisation_rules_v3_2.CN6 for the pattern-based rule and the prefix table covering all 14 supported Malaysian banks. Suppressed entries must be noted in observations.concerns with count + total amount + prefix.

Top parties size: top_payers and top_payees arrays MUST contain up to 10 entries (was 5 before v3.3.1). Schema enforces maxItems=10 and rank max=10. HTML renderer heading is 'Top 10 Payers (Income)' and 'Top 10 Payees (Outflow)'. Producing only 5 entries while >5 distinct counterparties exist is a compliance failure.

Parser quality grade driver (v3.3.1): use effective_match_rate = (pattern_matched + special_bucket) / total, NOT pattern_match_rate. special_bucket entries (AUTOPAY CHARGES, OTHER TRANSFER FEE, CASH CHQ DR, HSE CHQ DEPOSIT, HOUSE CHQ DR, CLRG CHQ DR, CDM CASH DEPOSIT) are correct handling — they legitimately have no counterparty beyond the bucket label. Grade A ≥ 95% effective, B ≥ 80%, C ≥ 60%, D ≥ 40%, F otherwise or any balance-trail FAIL.

C01.name: Own Party Credit
C01.description: Credits from the SAME legal entity — inter-account transfers between the company's own bank accounts.
C01.matching_rule: FULL COMPANY NAME MATCH after normalisation. NOT short root. Short root matching (MUHAFIZ, DMC) is used ONLY for related party detection (RP2 → C03/C04), NOT for own party (C01/C02).
C01.regex: (IBG CREDIT|DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT|TRANSFER TO A/C|I-FUNDS TR FROM).*{company_name_normalised}
exclusions: Sender is a DIFFERENT entity (even with similar name prefix)
Already classified as C03 (related party)
Do NOT rely only on transfer keywords — counterparty name is the classifier
C01.fallback: If no match → leave as 3rd party income (stays in net credits)
C01.cross_ref: Cannot also be C03. C01 takes priority over C03 if company name matches.

C02.name: Own Party Debit
C02.description: Debits transferring money TO the same company's other bank accounts. Mirror C01 logic for debit side. FULL COMPANY NAME MATCH.
C02.matching_rule: FULL COMPANY NAME MATCH after normalisation. NOT short root.
C02.dual_tag_note: When own-party debit contains loan keyword → tag C02 + C11. Excluded from net debits ONCE via C02.
exclusions: Recipient is a DIFFERENT entity (related party)
C02.fallback: If no match → check related party (C04)
C02.cross_ref: Can co-exist with C11. Own-party exclusion from net debits already covers it.

C03.name: Related Party Credit
C03.description: Credits from related entities — directors, shareholders, sister companies, family members. Purpose keyword is the tiebreaker: same person can be both related party AND customer.
C03.matching_rule: Match incoming credit sender name against related_parties[] list.
C03.trigger_note: When sender is a confirmed related party AND purpose contains trigger keyword → C03.
exclusions: Own-party (C01 takes priority)
Purpose indicates customer payment (e.g. 'Umrah payment' from director = customer, not C03)
Planworth AUTOPAY CR without F ADVANCE/ADVANCE keyword = NOT C03 (stays in net credits)
C03.fallback: If uncertain customer vs related party → classify as 3rd party income, flag for review
C03.cross_ref: Must not overlap with C01. C01 checked first.

C04.name: Related Party Debit
C04.impact: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately). v3.3: C04 is a reporting/tagging category only — like C11. Transactions are identified, listed in own_related_transactions, and flagged in Flag 11, but remain in net debits for conservative credit assessment.
C04.description: Debits paid TO related entities — directors, sister companies, family members.
C04.matching_rule: Match outgoing debit recipient against related_parties[] list. Rule order: C02 → C05 → C04.
exclusions: Own-party (C02 takes priority)
Salary keyword present (C05 takes priority)
Statutory payments
MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN operational expenses (visa, tickets) = regular expense per Q8

purpose_disambiguation.description: Purpose keyword after * disambiguates same person across categories.

examples.purpose: Repayment / Instalment / Housing Loan / Petty Cash / Credit Card

C04.fallback: If related party name found but purpose unclear → classify as C04 (conservative)
C04.cross_ref: Must not overlap with C02 or C05. Check C02 first, then C05, then C04.
C04.v3_3_change: C04 no longer excluded from net debits. Rationale: credit assessment should be conservative — understate income, overstate costs. Excluding related party debits flatters the borrower's cash flow by hiding director withdrawals, personal loans, and intercompany transfers. The analyst should see the full cost picture. C04 tagging, listing, and Flag 11 reporting all remain unchanged.

C05.name: Salary / Payroll
C05.impact: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)
C05.description: Salary and wage payments to employees.
C05.salary_regex_note: GAJI must be matched with word boundary (\bGAJI\b) to avoid MENGAJI/NGAJI (Malay: teaching) substring collisions on tuition/education business statements.

individual.note: NOT auto-salary. Extract name → check C04 → check salary keyword → if match = C05 → else regular expense.
Salary keyword in purpose after * required.

exclusions: AUTOPAY CHARGES (bank fee → C24)
AUTOPAY CR (incoming credit, not salary)
Commission payments to individuals (Comm, Commission, Komisen, Habuan) — see commission_policy_v3_3_1 below
C05.inclusions_note: STAFF INCENTIVE, STAFF OVERTIME, STAFF BONUS, STAFF ADVANCE, EXTRA SALARY = all C05 per Q10 decision.

commission_policy_v3_3_1.description: v3.3.1 NEW: recurring commission-style payments to individuals are NOT C05 by default. Commission agents are typically independent contractors, not employees. This applies to all banks and all business models (tuition academies, MLM, insurance agencies, real-estate agencies, freelance platforms, etc.). Driven by MYTUTOR ACADEMY validation run where 1,852 'Comm' tutor payments distorted EPF ratio to 46.91%.
commission_policy_v3_3_1.default_classification: Regular expense (NOT excluded from net debits)
commission_policy_v3_3_1.override: If user confirms (via pre-analysis gate Step 5) that agents are on company payroll as employees → reclassify as C05. User confirmation is REQUIRED — do not assume based on keyword alone.
commission_policy_v3_3_1.trigger_signal: When commission_keyword count > 20% of individual-transfer DR volume at the pre-analysis gate, pause and ask the user to confirm employment model before proceeding.
commission_policy_v3_3_1.impact_expectation: For pure commission businesses, salary_paid will be near-zero → salary_months_list empty → statutory coverage % is N/A (not 0%). Flag 6/7 should reflect 'no payroll to cover' rather than 'gaps detected'. Business model should be called out in observations.concerns.

C05.fallback: If TRANSFER FR A/C [name]* without salary keyword → NOT salary
C05.cross_ref: Salary takes priority over C04 if salary keyword present. Directors can receive both salary AND other payments. Commission payments never take C05 without explicit user confirmation (v3.3.1).

C06.name: Statutory - EPF
C06.impact: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)
C06.description: Employees Provident Fund (KWSP / Kumpulan Wang Simpanan Pekerja). v3.1 adds MONTH-BY-MONTH coverage check. v3.3.1 adds MANDATORY side-gate (see side_gate_v3_3_1 below) to prevent CR-side / own-party transfers being mis-tagged as EPF.

side_gate_v3_3_1.description: MANDATORY pre-check before keyword match. Applies to ALL banks. Driven by MUHAFIZ Feb 2026 bug where RM 600K DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT EPF PAYMENT <company_name> (CR side) was mis-tagged C06.
side_gate_v3_3_1.rule: if side == 'CR': NOT C06 (check C01/C03/other). if match_own_party(desc): C01/C02 (own-party earmarked funding transfer), NOT C06. Only after BOTH gates pass may the keyword match fire.
side_gate_v3_3_1.own_party_check: Uses FULL COMPANY NAME match after normalisation (strip SDN/BHD/& CO/(M)/PTY/LTD, collapse whitespace, uppercase). Bank-agnostic.

C06.keyword_priority: Search full Malay name FIRST, then abbreviations.
exclusions: Own-party transfers mentioning EPF (inter-account transfer to fund EPF → C01, not C06)
JomPAY without entity name visible
CR-side transactions mentioning KWSP/EPF — C06 is DR-only by definition (employer paying OUT to KWSP). Inbound credits with KWSP references are NOT EPF contributions.

Reference-number patterns like KWSP<digits> (e.g. KWSP0559246) embedded in transaction refs — these are KWSP Account 2 medical claim IDs disbursed by TPAs (PM CARE SDN BHD, MiCARE, Health Connect, etc.) to medical providers, NOT EPF employer contributions. Classify as normal revenue/income.

validation.coverage_check_primary: For each month: if salary_paid > 0 but statutory_epf = 0 → GAP. Report: epf_months_paid / salary_months_active × 100. List all missing months explicitly.
validation.ratio_check_secondary: EPF ÷ Salary per month: 8-16% = OK, outside = WARNING, > 16% = CATCH_UP. NEVER compute aggregate ratio across all months.

C06.fallback: If not detected but salary exists in ANY month → flag WARNING with list of missing months. Do NOT silently pass because aggregate ratio looks acceptable.

C07.name: Statutory - SOCSO
C07.impact: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)
C07.description: Social Security (PERKESO / Pertubuhan Keselamatan Sosial). v3.1: SOCSO is mandatory regardless of salary quantum — previous 'salary > RM50K' threshold removed. v3.3.1: inherits MANDATORY side-gate from C06.side_gate_v3_3_1 (side==DR AND NOT match_own_party) — applies equally to all banks.
C07.truncation_note: Maybank truncates to 'PERTUBUHAN KESELAMAT' (~20 chars); some banks truncate further to 'PERTUBUHAN KESELAMA' (lost final T). RHB FPX B2B prints 'FPX B2B PERTUBUH' (one-token cap) and statement column may further truncate to bare 'PERTUBUH CP_xxx' (no FPX B2B prefix). Regex matches all four forms while still rejecting unrelated 'PERTUBUHAN PELADANG / PELANCONGAN / NELAYAN / BUKAN KERAJAAN' organisations.
exclusions: JomPAY without entity name visible

CR-side transactions mentioning SOCSO/PERKESO — C07 is DR-only by definition (employer paying OUT to PERKESO). Inbound credits referencing SOCSO/PERKESO are refunds, claim disbursements, or welfare-fund payments (e.g. TABUNG PRIHATIN PROTEK PERKESO), NOT statutory contributions. Classify as revenue/other income.

validation.coverage_check_primary: For each month: if salary_paid > 0 but statutory_socso = 0 → GAP. Report: socso_months_paid / salary_months_active × 100. List all missing months explicitly.
validation.ratio_check_secondary: SOCSO ÷ Salary per month: 1-5% = OK, outside = WARNING, > 5% = CATCH_UP. NEVER compute aggregate ratio across all months.

C07.fallback: If not detected but salary exists in ANY month → flag WARNING with list of missing months.

C08.name: Statutory - Tax (LHDN)
C08.impact: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)
C08.description: Income tax (LHDN / Lembaga Hasil Dalam Negeri). v3.3.1: inherits MANDATORY side-gate from C06.side_gate_v3_3_1 (side==DR AND NOT match_own_party) — applies equally to all banks.
critical_exclusions: SERVICE TAX 8% SST (bank SST → C24, not income tax)
Person names containing 'HASIL' (e.g. HASILA BINTI HASHIM = customer, NOT LHDN)
C08.matching_note: Must match full phrase 'LEMBAGA HASIL DALAM NEGERI' or abbreviation 'LHDN'. Do NOT match partial 'HASIL'.
C08.fallback: If LHDN not detected across entire period but salary exists → flag WARNING. May legitimately be paid from different account.

C09.name: Statutory - HRDF
C09.impact: NOT excluded from Net Debits (reported separately)
C09.description: Human Resources Development Fund (PSMB / Pembangunan Sumber Manusia Berhad). v3.3.1: inherits MANDATORY side-gate from C06.side_gate_v3_3_1 (side==DR AND NOT match_own_party) — applies equally to all banks.
exclusions: JomPAY without entity name visible
C09.fallback: Often missing for small companies — not flagged as error

C10.name: Loan Disbursement / Factoring
C10.description: Loan proceeds and factoring/invoice financing advances credited to account.

tier_1.factoring_rule: F ADVANCE or ADVANCE + entity in known_factoring_entities[]

tier_2.rule: ADVANCE from unrecognised entity → flag for review

C10.planworth_rule: Only classify as C10 when description contains 'F ADVANCE' or 'ADVANCE'. AUTOPAY CR from factoring company WITHOUT advance keyword = potential surplus refund, stays in net credits.
C10.bank_entity_rule: Credit from bank ≠ automatic C10. Description keyword determines: FINANCING DISB = C10, REFUND = not C10, PROFIT PAYMENT = C12.
exclusions: Customer advance payments (one-off, small)
Staff advances returned (e.g. 'Advance DMC' from staff → check C03)
Factoring AUTOPAY CR without F ADVANCE/ADVANCE keyword
C10.fallback: If ADVANCE keyword but entity unknown → flag for review

C11.name: Loan Repayment
C11.description: Loan/financing repayments. Dual-tag with C02 allowed. C11 is for REPORTING. v3.2 SCOPE: C11 is for the COMPANY's own loan facilities ONLY.
C11.regex: \bTERM LOAN\b|\bLOAN REPAY|\bFINANCING REPAY|\bMONTHLY INSTALMENT\b|\bIB2G\s+DR\s+CA\s+CR\s+LN\b|\bTRANSFER TO LOAN\b|\bDD CASA PYMT\b|\bFINPAL ISSUER REPAYM

keyword_notes.IB2G DR CA CR LN: Alliance Bank internal book transfer: Debit Current Account, Credit Loan account. Unambiguous loan repayment signal.
keyword_notes.TRANSFER TO LOAN: Alliance Bank explicit wording; loan account number typically suffixed with 'L'.
keyword_notes.DD CASA PYMT: Direct debit from CASA (current/savings) to external loan issuer. Narrow to 'DD CASA PYMT <BANK/LENDER>' if false positives emerge from non-loan direct debits.
keyword_notes.FINPAL ISSUER REPAYM: FinPal (fintech issuer) loan repayment — specific string, no false-positive risk.

scope_clarification_v3_2.companys_own_loan: company_name + loan keyword → C02 + C11 dual-tag. The company is repaying its own financing facility.
scope_clarification_v3_2.directors_personal_loan: related_party_name + instalment/loan keyword → C04 ONLY (not C11). The company is paying someone's personal loan on their behalf.
scope_clarification_v3_2.test: If the entity name in the description is a RELATED PARTY (not the company itself), it CANNOT be C11. C04 wins.

examples.description: TR IBG MUHAFIZ SECURITY SDN Term loan
TR IBG SAMSI BIN IBRAHIM Monthly Instalment
TR IBG SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAMS Instalment
IB2G DR CA CR LN AOBFTR123456
examples.result: C11 (Alliance internal book transfer to own loan account)
C11 (Alliance direct transfer to loan facility)

examples.description: DD CASA PYMT BOOST BANK BERHAD 123456 BESTLITE ELECTRI
examples.result: C11 (direct debit repayment to Boost Bank loan)

examples.description: NBPS IBG DR CA AOBJOM... FINPAL ISSUER REPAYM

C11.other_transfer_fee_exclusion: OTHER TRANSFER FEE entries (typically RM0.10) are ALWAYS C24 regardless of purpose text. 'OTHER TRANSFER FEE Term loan' = C24, not C11.
C11.dual_tag_rule: Company's own loan: company_name + loan keyword → C02 + C11. Director's personal loan: related_party_name + instalment → C04 only. Account-number-only transfer (no entity name in description, e.g. 'TRANSFER TO LOAN 0000140820052291232L') → C11 standalone, NOT C02+C11 — the own-party match requires a full company name, and an account number alone doesn't satisfy it. These still flow through net debits as real cash outflow (the conservative treatment).

account_number_only_rule_v3_5_3.description: Cross-bank: transfer verbs + pure account-number/ID, no counterparty name. Classify as C11 standalone.
examples: NBPS IBG DR CA AOBJOM... FINPAL ISSUER REPAYM (Alliance/FinPal)
account_number_only_rule_v3_5_3.rationale: The account IS the company's own (facility is in company's name), but description carries only a numeric ID so the FULL COMPANY NAME match for C02 cannot succeed. Keep in net debits as conservative; still count in loan_repayment_dr and Flag 12. If parallel transactions for the same facility carry the company name, tag them C02+C11 per-transaction.

C11.jompay_note: Recurring same biller code monthly = likely loan repayment, but only if clearly defined or entity name visible.
exclusions: TR IBG [related party] Instalment → C04 (director's personal loan), NOT C11
OTHER TRANSFER FEE [any keyword] → C24 (bank fee), NOT C11. Amount typically RM0.10
Personal loans of directors/family members paid by company → C04 only, never C11
C11.fallback: If JomPAY biller code unknown → leave as regular expense

C12.name: FD / Interest Income
C12.description: Fixed deposit maturities, sweep-in, interest/profit credits. Credit side only.
C12.regex: (?:FD MATUR|FIXED DEPOSIT|FD UPLIFT|INTEREST CREDIT|PROFIT CREDIT|SWEEP IN|\bHIBAH\b|DIVIDEND PAID)
C12.note: Same keywords on DEBIT side = C11 (loan repayment), NOT C12. Islamic banks use PROFIT/HIBAH instead of INTEREST.

C13.name: Reversal Credit
C13.description: Bank reversals — credits reversing a previous erroneous debit.
exclusions: IBG INWARD RETURN → C16
GIRO INWARD RETURN → C16

C14.name: Returned Cheques Inward
C14.description: Cheques deposited that bounced. Bank debits to reverse the earlier credit.

C15.name: Returned Cheques Outward
C15.description: Company's own cheques bounced. Major red flag if detected.
C15.keywords: Same as C14 but CREDIT side

C16.name: IBG/GIRO Inward Return
C16.description: Failed outward transfers returning money to account. Money never actually left.
C16.note: Separate from C13 (true bank reversals). Both excluded from net credits. Decision per Q1 (Meesha).

C17.name: Cash Deposit
C17.description: Physical cash deposits. AML monitoring — track frequency.
exclusions: CASH CHQ DR (withdrawal → C18)
C17.aml_note: Count frequency for AML monitoring tools.

C18.name: Cash Withdrawal
C18.description: Physical cash withdrawn via cash cheque.
C18.distinction: CASH CHQ DR = C18 (cash withdrawal). HOUSE CHQ DR / CLRG CHQ DR = C20 (cheque issue). Prefix before 'CHQ DR' distinguishes.

C19.name: Cheque Deposit
C19.regex: (?:HSE CHQ DEPOSIT|2D LOCAL CHQ|CHQ DEPOSIT|CHEQUE DEPOSIT)
C19.counterparty_rule: Counterparty = 'Unidentified (Cheque)'. NEVER use cheque labels as top party name.

C20.name: Cheque Issue
C20.regex: (?:HOUSE CHQ DR|CLRG CHQ DR|INWARD CLEARING CHQ DEBIT)
exclusions: CASH CHQ DR → C18
CHQ PROCESSING FEE → C24

C21.name: Round Figure Credit
C21.description: Credits that are exact multiples of RM10,000. Structuring/AML indicator.
C21.note: Pure math — no keyword matching. Applied AFTER classification.

C22.name: High Value Credit
C22.description: Credits > 3× monthly average EOD balance. Relative threshold — changes monthly.
C22.fallback: If EOD average unavailable or unreliable (reconciliation FAIL) → SKIP C22 entirely. No proxy values.
C22.distinction: C22 = relative (3× EOD, system-calculated). C23 = absolute fixed threshold.

C23.name: Large Credit
C23.distinction: C23 = absolute fixed threshold. C22 = relative (3× EOD).

C24.name: Bank Fees & Charges
C24.description: Service charges, processing fees, SST. Valid operating costs.
C24.regex: (?:AUTOPAY CHARGES|OTHER TRANSFER FEE|CH(?:Q|EQUE)\s+PROCESS(?:ING)?\s+FEE|3RD PARTY CHEQUE|ACCOUNT STATUS CONFIRM|SERVICE TAX \d+%|STAMP DUTY|CABLE CHARGE|NOSTRO CHARGE|MAS SERVICE CHARGE|AGENT CHARGES|CMS - DR CORP CHG|HANDLING\s+CHRG|CHEQ(?:UE)?\s+STAMP\s+FEE|RFLX\s+INSTANT\s+TRF\s+SC|RFLX\s*/\s*CM\d+|REFLEX-\s*/\s*CM\d+|CHQ\s+SVC|SERVICE\s+CASH\s+CHQ|SERVICE\s+CHARGES-OTHERS|BANKERS\s+REFER\s+CHARGES)
C24.other_transfer_fee_rule_v3_2: OTHER TRANSFER FEE entries (typically RM0.10) are ALWAYS C24 regardless of what purpose text follows. If amount <= RM1.00 and description starts with 'OTHER TRANSFER FEE' → C24, full stop.
C24.note: Small amounts. Must not be misclassified as salary, statutory, or any exclusion category.

C25.name: Balance Row (Exclude)
C25.description: Statement balance markers. Filter FIRST before any classification.

dual_purpose.banks_with_header: C25 rows are duplicates — extract balances from header, remove C25 rows.
dual_purpose.banks_without_header: C25 rows ARE the source of opening/closing balance — extract amounts FIRST then remove from transaction list.
dual_purpose.fallback: If no header AND no C25 rows → opening/closing balance unavailable, reconciliation cannot run.

C26.name: Trade Income
C26.description: v3.4 NEW. CR from a third-party company (customer payment for goods/services). Eliminates the 'RM millions unclassified CR' blind spot for operating businesses.
triggers: counterparty contains SDN BHD / BHD / BERHAD / ENTERPRISE / TRADING / CORPORATION / GROUP / HOLDINGS / INDUSTRIES
examples: PIASAU GAS SDN BHD (Muhafiz client receipt)
SCHENKER LOGISTICS (M) SDN. BHD. (Muhafiz client receipt)
SOUTHERN CABLE SDN B (Muhafiz client receipt)
C26.note: Natural-person counterparties (BIN/BINTI/A/L/A/P names) do NOT fire C26 — they are not trade partners by default. Such rows remain unclassified unless C03/C04/C05/C11 fires.

C27.name: Trade Expense
C27.description: v3.4 NEW. DR to a third-party company (vendor payment for goods/services). Symmetrical to C26 Trade Income.
triggers: counterparty contains SDN BHD / BHD / BERHAD / ENTERPRISE / TRADING / CORPORATION / GROUP / HOLDINGS / INDUSTRIES
C27.note: Natural-person counterparties do NOT fire C27. Such rows remain unclassified unless another DR category (C02/C04/C05/C10/C11/C18/C19/C20/C24) fires.

RP1.name: User Override
RP1.description: If user provides related_parties[] list, use as PRIMARY source. Highest authority.

RP2.name: Root Name Match
RP2.description: Extract distinctive/unique word(s) from company_name. Other entities sharing root → Sister Company CANDIDATE. Root = distinctive word regardless of length. Short but unique (DMC) = sufficient. Short but generic (GLOBAL, PRIME) = needs more words.
RP2.note: Root match = CANDIDATE only. Validate by transaction frequency/pattern. Used for C03/C04 related party detection, NOT for C01/C02 own party matching.

RP3.name: Account Holder as Payee
RP3.description: Person names in recurring debits with personal keywords → Director. Two-way financial behaviour = potential related party for analyst review.

RP4.name: Recurring Personal Payee
RP4.description: Individual names in 3+ debits with personal keywords (PETTY CASH, CREDIT CARD, HOUSING LOAN). Frequency alone ≠ related party. Frequency + personal keywords = strong signal.

RP5.name: Fuzzy Name Match
RP5.description: Same person with different name formats → resolve to one entity. 3-tier deterministic matching.

RP6.name: Exclusion List
RP6.description: Never auto-detect as related parties: govt (JANM/LHDN/KWSP/PERKESO/PSMB), banks, utilities, known suppliers, factoring companies.
examples: HALA GATEWAYS = supplier (17 txns, RM516K), NOT related

RP7.name: Transaction Purpose Detection
RP7.description: v3.2 NEW: Entities receiving debits with purposes SHARE CAPITAL, SHARE CAP, OPENING CA, MTSB SHARE CAP → Sister Company (confirmed). These are equity/investment transactions that definitively indicate corporate relationships.
RP7.note: Overrides RP6 exclusion list only for SHARE CAPITAL keywords. These are definitively related party indicators.

RP8.name: Surname-Based Family Detection
RP8.description: v3.2 NEW: Extract surname (last token) from each known director name. Search all transaction counterparties for OTHER person names containing this surname AND having personal-purpose transactions (INSTALMENT, MONTHLY INSTALMENT, loan references) → Family Member.
requirements: Surname must be distinctive (not common names like BIN, BINTI, AHMAD alone)
Counterparty must have personal-purpose keywords, not just surname match
RP8.example: Director = SHAHARUDDIN BIN SAMSI → surname 'SAMSI' → find SAMSI BIN IBRAHIM with Monthly Instalment → Family Member

DMC.name: NUR THAHIRAH NAZIHA
DMC.source: Q7 — Luqman confirmed. CR RM98,342 (C03) + DR RM144,803 (C04)

CP1.method: Skip 'IBG CREDIT', skip ref codes (<8 chars alphanumeric), take remaining words as entity name.

CP2.method: Factoring advance. Extract company name after 'F ADVANCE'. Normalise FAC → FACTORING.

CP3.method: Skip 'DUITNOW TO ACCOUNT', skip purpose words (ONLINE TRANSFER, CASH, PAYMENT), extract entity name.

CP4.method: Skip 'AUTOPAY CR', skip batch ref (duplicated), take entity name.

CP7.method: Skip 'TRANSFER TO A/C', take name before *, purpose after *. Truncation handled by RP5.

CP6.method: Skip prefix, extract entity/person name. Use stop rules (numbers, MBB, CT) not fixed word count.

CP8.method: Skip 'TRANSFER FR A/C', take name before *, purpose after *.

CP9.method: Skip 'PAYMENT FR A/C', skip card/account number, extract entity after *. Check statutory (C06/C07) first.

CP10.method: Cannot extract counterparty. Tag as 'Unidentified (Cheque)'. NEVER use as top party name.

CP11.method: Simple cleanup (punctuation, uppercase, SDN/BHD) can be coded. Merging truncated names needs AI. Only normalise when confident. Wrong normalisation worse than duplicates.

CN1.name: Factoring Entity Consolidation
CN1.description: Merge all PLANWORTH GLOBAL variants (PLANWORTH GLOBAL FAC, PLANWORTH GLOBAL FACTORING) → 'PLANWORTH GLOBAL'. Do NOT list 'FAC' as a separate entity.

CN2.name: Fragment Suppression
CN2.description: If an extracted name is clearly a suffix/fragment of a known longer entity name, merge it with the parent.

CN3.name: JANM Consolidation
CN3.description: All 'JANM CAWANGAN [location]' variants → merge to 'JANM' for top party ranking. Individual branches tracked in transaction detail only.

CN4.name: Same Entity Different Purposes
CN4.description: When same entity appears with different payment descriptions, merge by entity name. Strip purpose suffix for top party display.

CN5.name: Related Parties in Top Payees
CN5.description: Related parties appearing in top payees MUST show canonical name from related_parties[] and be marked is_related_party=true.

CN6.name: Ghost-Verb Suppression (cross-bank)
CN6.description: v3.3.1 MANDATORY: counterparty entries that are ONLY a payment-rail prefix with no entity name attached are parser dropouts. They MUST be EXCLUDED from top_parties.top_payers and top_parties.top_payees. This is a cross-bank rule — applies to ALL 14 supported banks and to any new bank added later.
CN6.rule_pattern: A counterparty name is a ghost verb if, after normalisation (strip SDN/BHD/& CO/(M)/PTY/LTD, collapse whitespace, uppercase), the entire normalised string matches one of the bank-specific prefixes below AND has no additional alphabetic token of ≥3 letters other than those in the stopword set.
CN6.generic_fallback: For any bank not listed above, or any new bank added later: if the counterparty name has no alphabetic token of ≥3 letters OTHER than the stopword set [TRANSFER, PAYMENT, IBG, IB2G, IBFT, IBK, CR, DR, CREDIT, DEBIT, TO, FR, FROM, A/C, C/A, ACCOUNT, ACCT, INTER, BANK, BANKING, INTO, ONLINE, DUITNOW, DUIT, NOW, FPX, RENTAS, REMITTANCE, ELECTRONIC, AUTOPAY, INSTANT, FAST, OUTWARD, INWARD, OUTW, INW, OUT, IN, ADVICE, TRF, BLKTRF, NBPS, TR, PYMT, PAY, THE, AND, OF, FOR, WITH, SA, CA, CCARD, CARD, CHQ, CHEQUE, CASH, DEPOSIT, WITHDRAWAL, HSE, HOUSE, CLRG, CDM, 2D, LOCAL, GIR, GIRO, HLB, MBB, RHB, ABB, PBB, BIMB, AMB, AMBANK, PBE, CIMB, OCBC, UOB, BSN, PMT, SLRY] — it is a ghost verb. This is the canonical cross-bank stopword set; renderer and classifier MUST use the same list. Do NOT add to stopwords: HONG, LEONG, PUBLIC, ALLIANCE, RAKYAT, ISLAM, ISLAMIC, BERHAD, NEGARA, AGRO, AFFIN, MAYBANK, MUAMALAT — these can appear inside real entity names and must remain classifiable.
CN6.cheque_patterns_note: Cheque patterns (HSE CHQ DEPOSIT, CDM CASH DEPOSIT, 2D LOCAL CHQ, CASH CHQ DR, HOUSE CHQ DR, CLRG CHQ DR) are aggregated into C17/C18/C19/C20 — they must NOT appear as named counterparties in top_parties either. Same suppression rule applies.
CN6.reporting: When any counterparty is suppressed by CN6, add a note to observations.concerns stating count + total amount + prefix affected. Example: '206 debit transactions totalling RM 44,224.48 under TRANSFER FR A/C could not be attributed to a counterparty (parser dropped the name) — excluded from top payees ranking.'

closed_questions.question: IBG/GIRO Inward Return — exclude from net credits?
closed_questions.decision: YES — exclude. C16 is separate from C13 but both excluded from net credits. Added as Step 7 in net credits formula.
closed_questions.impact:
MUHAFIZ: RM18,622 (9 txns)
DMC: RM780 (1 txn)

closed_questions.question: CREDIT ADV (RM4,504, no description) — how to classify?
closed_questions.decision: Option A with tracking. Unclassified stays in net credits/debits. Track monthly (unclassified_cr_count/amount, unclassified_dr_count/amount). List individually when > RM10K (user-configurable).

closed_questions.question: TR TO SAVINGS (91 txns, RM316K) — all salary?
closed_questions.decision: Option C — NOT auto-salary. AUTOPAY DR captures bulk salary. Classify individually: extract name → check related party (C04) → check salary keywords → if match = C05 → remainder = regular expense.

closed_questions.question: Planworth AUTOPAY CR without 'F ADVANCE' — exclude all?
closed_questions.decision: Option B — only with 'F ADVANCE'/'ADVANCE'. AUTOPAY CR without advance keyword = potential surplus refund, stays in net credits. Applies to all factoring companies.

closed_questions.question: SHAUFIAH NUR ASHIKIN (6 txns RM19K) — relationship?
closed_questions.decision: Family Member — mother of SHAHARUDDIN (director). All 6 txns = C04.

closed_questions.question: DAYANG SITI RAUDZAH (33 txns RM179K) — relationship?
closed_questions.decision: Family Member — wife of SHAHARUDDIN (director). All 33 txns = C04. Total RM179,632 (not RM44K as originally listed — TR TO SAVINGS added 21 more).

closed_questions.question: NUR THAHIRAH NAZIHA (90 txns, RM244K) — who?
MUHAMMAD SIDDIQ BIN (57 txns, RM190K) — classification?
closed_questions.decision: Option C — split. Salary (RM3,089.15/month) = C05. Operational (visa, tickets) = regular expense. Credits 'Advance'/'Advance DMC' = C03. Credits 'Refund'/'Return' = excluded. RP3 behavioural detection.

closed_questions.question: TT CREDIT WITHOUT FLOAT (RM71,590) — is this FX?
closed_questions.decision: Do NOT assume FX. TT = transfer method, not currency indicator. Flag as potential FX for analyst review. Default to NOT FX unless clear evidence.

closed_questions.question: STAFF INCENTIVE (RM10K) — salary or bonus?
closed_questions.decision: Option A — include under C05. Staff incentive = employee compensation. STAFF INCENTIVE, STAFF OVERTIME, STAFF BONUS, STAFF ADVANCE, EXTRA SALARY = all C05.

Net Debits.notes: After exclusion of own_party_dr (C02) only. v3.3: C04 no longer excluded.

Salary (C05).notes: MUHAFIZ: AUTOPAY DR bulk. DMC: individual transfers

Tax (C08).notes: DMC: not detected (may be paid elsewhere)

FX.notes: DMC: flagged as potential FX per Q9, not confirmed

general_notes.text: THREE EXECUTION MODES: All 25 classification rules serve THREE modes from ONE rulebook: (1) Full AI — Claude classifies from raw descriptions. (2) Optimised AI — Python handles deterministic rules first, AI handles fuzzy judgment only. (3) Full Code — everything in Python, zero AI. Execution Mode column indicates readiness: FULL CODE = works in all 3 modes. AI ASSIST = needs AI in modes 1 & 2, exhaustive rules needed for mode 3.

JOMPAY GLOBAL RULE: JomPAY is a payment channel, NOT a payee. Never classify based on JomPAY biller code alone. Only classify when entity name is visible in description. If description is just 'JOMPAY xxxx:xxxxx' with no entity name → leave as regular expense. Applies to C06, C07, C08, C09, and C11.

CLASSIFICATION RULE ORDER: C25 (filter balance rows) → C01/C02 (own party) → C05 (salary) → C03/C04 (related party) → C06-C09 (statutory) → C10 (loan disb) → C11 (loan repay) → C12-C13 (FD/reversal) → C14-C16 (returned cheques/returns) → C17-C20 (cash/cheque) → C24 (bank fees) → remainder = income/expense
NO 'UNKNOWN' BUCKET: If no exclusion matches, transaction is income (credit side) or expense (debit side). It stays in net credits/debits.
DUAL-TAGGING: A single transaction can carry two category tags (e.g. C02 + C11). Excluded from net debits only ONCE via C02. C11 tracked separately for reporting.
COMPANY-AGNOSTIC: Rules use variables (company_name, related_parties[], known_factoring_entities[]) — not hardcoded names. Test data: MUHAFIZ (CIMB) and DMC (Maybank Islamic).

UNCLASSIFIED TRANSACTIONS: Description too vague/missing to determine type. Track monthly: unclassified_cr_count, unclassified_cr_amount, unclassified_dr_count, unclassified_dr_amount. List individually when single transaction > RM10,000 (user-configurable). Stays in net credits/debits — NOT excluded.

FX DEFAULT RULE: Default to NOT FX unless clear conversion evidence exists. TT CREDIT = transfer method, NOT currency indicator. RENTAS/JANM = domestic MYR. Alphabet codes (GBPV, USDP) in reference fields = internal voucher codes, NOT currencies.

v3.1 STATUTORY COVERAGE VALIDATION: Statutory validation uses TWO layers. Layer 1 (PRIMARY) — Coverage Check: For every month where salary_paid > 0, check whether each statutory field (EPF, SOCSO, LHDN) is also > 0. Missing = GAP. Layer 2 (SECONDARY) — Per-Month Ratio Check: Only where BOTH salary and statutory exist in same month. EPF 8-16% OK, SOCSO 1-5% OK. Ratios above range may indicate lump-sum catch-up. NEVER compute ratio as total_statutory / total_salary across all months — this masks gaps. Output in consolidated.statutory_compliance with missing month lists and per-month ratios. See V7-V10 in Tab 6.

v3.1 SOCSO FALLBACK FIX: Previous threshold 'salary > RM50K' for SOCSO warning was too lenient. SOCSO is mandatory regardless of salary quantum. Removed the RM50K threshold. Now: if salary_paid > 0 in ANY month but statutory_socso = 0 → flag WARNING with missing month list.

v3.2 C11 SCOPE: C11 is for the COMPANY's own loan facilities ONLY. Director/family member personal loans (instalment, housing loan) paid by company = C04 only, NEVER C11. Test: if entity name is a RELATED PARTY (not company itself), it CANNOT be C11.
v3.2 OTHER TRANSFER FEE: 'OTHER TRANSFER FEE' entries (typically RM0.10) are ALWAYS C24 (bank fee) regardless of purpose text that follows. 'OTHER TRANSFER FEE Term loan' = C24, not C11.

v3.2 RP7/RP8 AUTO-DETECTION: RP7 — entities receiving SHARE CAPITAL/SHARE CAP debits = Sister Company (confirmed). RP8 — surname-based family detection: extract director surname, find other persons with same surname + personal loan keywords = Family Member.

v3.2 COUNTERPARTY NORMALISATION: (1) Merge PLANWORTH GLOBAL variants (FAC → FACTORING). (2) Suppress fragments ('FAC' alone → merge into parent). (3) Merge JANM CAWANGAN variants → 'JANM' for top party. (4) Same entity, different purposes → merge by entity name. (5) Related parties in top payees must show canonical name + is_related_party=true.

v3.2 DETERMINISTIC EOD: Walk transactions in statement order, group by date, EOD = last transaction's running balance per date. eod_lowest=MIN, eod_highest=MAX, eod_average=SUM/COUNT(distinct dates). Only dates with transactions count. Opening balance from previous month excluded.

v3.3 C04 EXCLUSION CHANGE: Related party debits (C04) are NO LONGER excluded from net debits. Net debits formula: gross_debits - own_party_dr(C02) only. C04 remains a reporting/tagging category — transactions are still classified, listed in own_related_transactions, and flagged in Flag 11. Rationale: conservative credit assessment — understate income, overstate costs. This also eliminates the consistency problem where name matching differences between AI runs would move net debit figures.
